In 2012, Intel reported that their tablet program improved productivity for about 19,000 of their employees by an average of 57 minutes a day.[[193]](#cite_note-193) In October 2012, display screen shipments for tablets began surpassing shipments for laptop display screens.[[194]](#cite_note-194) Tablets became increasingly used in the [construction industry](/wiki/Construction_industry "Construction industry") to look at [blueprints](/wiki/Blueprint "Blueprint"), field documentation and other relevant information on the device instead of carrying around large amounts of paper.[[195]](#cite_note-195) [*Time*](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)") described the product's popularity as a "global tablet craze" in a November 2012 article.[[196]](#cite_note-196)

As of the start of 2014, 44% of US online consumers owned tablets,[[197]](#cite_note-197) a significant jump from 5% in 2011.[[198]](#cite_note-198) Tablet use also became increasingly common among children. A 2014 survey found that mobiles were the most frequently used object for play among American children under the age of 12. Mobiles were used more often in play than video game consoles, board games, puzzles, play vehicles, blocks and dolls/action figures. Despite this, the majority of parents said that a mobile was "never" or only "sometimes" a toy.[[199]](#cite_note-199) As of 2014, nearly two-thirds of American 2- to 10-year-olds have access to a tablet or [e-reader](/wiki/E-reader "E-reader").[[200]](#cite_note-200) The large use of tablets by adults is as a personal internet-connected TV.[[201]](#cite_note-201) A 2015 study found that a third of children under five have their own tablet device.[[202]](#cite_note-202)

After a fast rise in sales during the early 2010s, the tablet market had plateaued in 2015[[203]](#cite_note-203) and by Q3 2018[[204]](#cite_note-idc2018-204)[[205]](#cite_note-idc2020,jan30-205) sales had declined by 35% from its Q3 2014 peak.[[206]](#cite_note-206) In spite of this, tablet sales worldwide had surpassed sales of desktop computers in 2017,[[207]](#cite_note-207) and worldwide PC sales were flat for the first quarter of 2018.[[208]](#cite_note-208) In 2020 the tablet market saw a large surge in sales with 164 million tablet units being shipped worldwide due to a large demand for [work from home](/wiki/Remote_work "Remote work") and [online learning](/wiki/Virtual_school "Virtual school").[[209]](#cite_note-209)

### By operating system

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tablet_computer&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: By operating system")]

According to a survey conducted by the Online Publishers Association (OPA) now called [Digital Content Next](/wiki/Digital_Content_Next "Digital Content Next") (DCN) in March 2012, it found that 72% of tablet owners had an iPad, while 32% had an [Android](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)") tablet. By 2012, Android tablet adoption had increased. 52% of tablet owners owned an iPad, while 51% owned an Android-powered tablet (percentages do not add up to 100% because some tablet owners own more than one type).[[237]](#cite_note-OwnerPercent-237) By end of 2013, Android's market share rose to 61.9%, followed by iOS at 36%.[[238]](#cite_note-238) By late 2014, Android's market share rose to 72%, followed by iOS at 22.3% and Windows at 5.7%.[[239]](#cite_note-239) As of early 2016, Android has 65% marketshare, Apple has 26% and Windows has 9% marketshare.[[81]](#cite_note-mspoweruser.com-81) In Q1 2018, Android tablets had 62% of the market, Apple's iOS had 23.4% of the market and [Windows 10](/wiki/Windows_10 "Windows 10") had 14.6% of the market.[[131]](#cite_note-auto-131)

## Use

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tablet_computer&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Use")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d5/Pocket_PC.jpg/250px-Pocket_PC.jpg)](/wiki/File:Pocket_PC.jpg)

A person using pocket pc

### Sleep

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tablet_computer&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Sleep")]

Main article: [Electronic media and sleep](/wiki/Electronic_media_and_sleep "Electronic media and sleep")

The blue wavelength of light from back-lit tablets may impact one's ability to fall asleep when reading at night, through the suppression of [melatonin](/wiki/Melatonin "Melatonin").[[241]](#cite_note-241) Experts at [Harvard Medical School](/wiki/Harvard_Medical_School "Harvard Medical School") suggest limiting tablets for reading use in the evening. Those who have a delayed body clock, such as teenagers, which makes them prone to stay up late in the evening and sleep later in the morning, may be at particular risk for increases in sleep deficiencies.[[242]](#cite_note-242) A PC app such as [F.lux](/wiki/F.lux "F.lux") and Android apps such as CF.lumen[[243]](#cite_note-243) and Twilight[[244]](#cite_note-244) attempt to decrease the impact on sleep by filtering blue wavelengths from the display. [iOS 9.3](/wiki/IOS_9.3 "IOS 9.3") includes [Night Shift](/wiki/Night_Shift_(software) "Night Shift (software)") that shifts the colors of the device's display to be warmer during the later hours.[[245]](#cite_note-245)

### By plane

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tablet_computer&action=edit&section=42 "Edit section: By plane")]

Because of, among other things, electromagnetic waves emitted by this type of device, the use of any type of electronic device during the take-off and landing phases was totally prohibited on board commercial flights. On November 13, 2013, the [European Aviation Safety Agency](/wiki/European_Aviation_Safety_Agency "European Aviation Safety Agency") (EASA) announced that the use of mobile terminals could be authorized on the flights of European airlines during these phases from 2014 onwards, on the condition that the cellular functions are deactivated ("airplane" mode activated).[[246]](#cite_note-246) In September 2014, EASA issued guidance that allows EU airlines to permit use of tablets, e-readers, smartphones, and other portable electronic devices to stay on without the need to be in airplane mode during all parts of EU flights; however, each airline has to decide to allow this behavior.[[247]](#cite_note-247) In the U.S., the [Federal Aviation Administration](/wiki/Federal_Aviation_Administration "Federal Aviation Administration") allowed use of portable electronic devices during all parts of flights while in airplane mode in late 2013.[[248]](#cite_note-248)

### Tourism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tablet_computer&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Tourism")]

Some French historical monuments are equipped with digital tactile tablets called "HistoPad".[[249]](#cite_note-249) It is an application integrated with an [iPad Mini](/wiki/IPad_Mini "IPad Mini") offering an interaction in augmented and virtual reality with several pieces of the visit, the visitor being able to take control of their visit in an interactive and personalized way.

### Professional use for specific sectors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tablet_computer&action=edit&section=44 "Edit section: Professional use for specific sectors")]

Some professionals – for example, in the construction industry, insurance experts, lifeguards or surveyors – use so-called rugged shelf models in the field that can withstand extreme hot or cold shocks or climatic environments. Some units are hardened against drops and screen breakage. Satellite-connectivity-equipped tablets such as the Thorium X,[[250]](#cite_note-250) for example, can be used in areas where there is no other connectivity. This is a valuable feature in the aeronautical and military realms. For example, United States Army helicopter pilots are moving to tablets as [electronic flight bags](/wiki/Electronic_flight_bag "Electronic flight bag"), which confer the advantages of rapid, convenient synchronization of large groups of users, and the seamless updating of information.[[251]](#cite_note-251) US Army chaplains who are deployed in the field with the troops cite the accessibility of Army regulations, field manuals, and other critical information to help with their services; however, power generation, speakers, and a tablet rucksack are also necessary for the chaplains.[[252]](#cite_note-252)

### Prisons

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tablet_computer&action=edit&section=45 "Edit section: Prisons")]

In the United States, specialized tablets made by [JPay](/wiki/JPay "JPay") and [Global Tel Link](/wiki/Global_Tel_Link "Global Tel Link") are available in many prisons. These tablets have been criticized by the [Prison Policy Initiative](/wiki/Prison_Policy_Initiative "Prison Policy Initiative") for exploitative [microtransactions](/wiki/Microtransactions "Microtransactions"), as well as for providing an excuse to cut other programs such as [prison libraries](/wiki/Prison_libraries "Prison libraries").[[253]](#cite_note-w381-253)[[254]](#cite_note-n972-254)[[255]](#cite_note-z390-255)
